CLAUDE CODE IS THE OPENCLAW ALTERNATIVE YOU ALREADY HAVE https://every.


ANTHROPIC SHIPPED IT OVER A YEAR AGO. MOST PEOPLE JUST DIDN'T NOTICE. https://every.

by Nityesh Agarwal https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiQ2xhdWRlIENvZGUgSXMgdGhlIE9wZW5DbGF3IEFsdGVybmF0aXZlIFlvdSBBbHJlYWR5IEhhdmUiLCJwb3N0X2lkIjo0MzIxLCJwb3N0X3R5cGUiOiJwb3N0IiwidXJsIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9ldmVyeS50by9Abml0eWVzaCIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjo0fQ==

Sign up https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiQ2xhdWRlIENvZGUgSXMgdGhlIE9wZW5DbGF3IEFsdGVybmF0aXZlIFlvdSBBbHJlYWR5IEhhdmUiLCJwb3N0X2lkIjo0MzIxLCJwb3N0X3R5cGUiOiJwb3N0IiwidXJsIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9ldmVyeS50by9hY2NvdW50IiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjV9 to get it in your inbox.



----------------------------------------



OpenClaw https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiQ2xhdWRlIENvZGUgSXMgdGhlIE9wZW5DbGF3IEFsdGVybmF0aXZlIFlvdSBBbHJlYWR5IEhhdmUiLCJwb3N0X2lkIjo0MzIxLCJwb3N0X3R5cGUiOiJwb3N0IiwidXJsIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9ldmVyeS50by9ndWlkZXMvY2xhdy1zY2hvb2wiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6Nn0= showed the world what an AI assistant could look like.

The open-source project became the most-starred software project in history in only 60 days https://every., not because of hype. People were experiencing AI that didnвҖҷt just answer questions but did things. Managed your calendar. Sent emails. Controlled your browser. All triggered from a text message in WhatsApp or Slack.

I watched Sam Altman hire OpenClawвҖҷs creator and Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella build вҖңClawPilotвҖқ on top of the framework. And the whole time, one thought kept nagging at me: Claude Code already does all of this. WhatвҖҷs so special about the crustaceans?

The answer was rooted in public perception. OpenClaw was marketed as an AI agent, and Claude Code was marketed as a coding tool.

WeвҖҷve now spent months building on both Claude Code and OpenClaw at EveryвҖ”including Claudie https://every., an AI employee who runs our consulting teamвҖҷs back office. That work has shown us why initial excitement about OpenClaw is cooling https://every.вҖ”linked to its unreliabilityвҖ”and why Claude Code is the most capable platform for building an AI assistant today.

If youвҖҷre trying to build an AI that does things, you donвҖҷt need to wait for a better OpenClaw alternative because you already have one. HereвҖҷs the case for it, and how to use it.


HARNESSING THE MODELS

Claude Code can do everything that made OpenClaw go viral: Use tools, manage files, and run for hours on its own. ThatвҖҷs not a coincidence because underneath, the two are the same thing: a harness for an AI model.

Think of AI modelsвҖ”Claude https://every., GPT https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiQ2xhdWRlIENvZGUgSXMgdGhlIE9wZW5DbGF3IEFsdGVybmF0aXZlIFlvdSBBbHJlYWR5IEhhdmUiLCJwb3N0X2lkIjo0MzIxLCJwb3N0X3R5cGUiOiJwb3N0IiwidXJsIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9ldmVyeS50by92aWJlLWNoZWNrL2dwdC01LTUiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6MTF9, Gemini https://every.вҖ”as powerful, capable horses. A harness is the thing that lets you direct that horsepower. ItвҖҷs the software layer that sits between a raw AI model and the task youвҖҷre trying to accomplish. It decides how the model receives context, which tools it can use, how it remembers things across conversations, and how it talks to the outside world.

YouвҖҷve been using harnesses already: ChatGPT wraps a model in a chat interface that can browse the web and run code; Cursor wraps one in a code editor. Claude Code wraps one in something more open-ended: the ability to call tools, chain steps together, and work autonomously toward a goal. Which is exactly what OpenClaw does.

CODEX FOR EVERYTHING AND EVERYONE https://every.


PLUS CLAUDE JOINS SLACK, AND DESIGN GETS ITS OWN AI TELLS. https://every.

by Laura Entis https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiQ29kZXggZm9yIEV2ZXJ5dGhpbmcgYW5kIEV2ZXJ5b25lIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMyMCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vQGxhdXJhXzI3YmJhZl8xIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjR9 and Katie Parrott https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiQ29kZXggZm9yIEV2ZXJ5dGhpbmcgYW5kIEV2ZXJ5b25lIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMyMCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vQGthdGllLnBhcnJvdHQxMiIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjo1fQ==

Midjourney/Every illustration.

Cutting-edge AI tools used to be the domain of engineers. No longer. Now the technology is accessible enough that anyone who wants to work more efficiently and ambitiously can use coding agents like Codex. With that in mind, staff writer Katie Parrott https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiQ29kZXggZm9yIEV2ZXJ5dGhpbmcgYW5kIEV2ZXJ5b25lIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMyMCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vQGthdGllLnBhcnJvdHQxMiIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjo2fQ== updates our guide to using Codex for knowledge work, head of social media Becky Isjwara https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiQ29kZXggZm9yIEV2ZXJ5dGhpbmcgYW5kIEV2ZXJ5b25lIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMyMCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vYmVja3lpc2ouY29tLyIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjo3fQ== shares her Codex hack for making YouTube thumbnails, and AnthropicвҖҷs new Slack agent, Claude Tag, validates the collaborative, shared-agent future Every has been living in since January.

WeвҖҷre hosting a live Codex for Power Users Camp https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiQ29kZXggZm9yIEV2ZXJ5dGhpbmcgYW5kIEV2ZXJ5b25lIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMyMCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vZXZlbnRzL2NvZGV4LXBvd2VyLXVzZXItY2FtcCIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjo4fQ== this Friday, June 26, for paid Every subscribers. During the two-hour event, the Every team will share how Codex has become a daily driver for writing, research, growth, customer support, and engineering. RSVP https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiQ29kZXggZm9yIEV2ZXJ5dGhpbmcgYW5kIEV2ZXJ5b25lIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMyMCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vZXZlbnRzL2NvZGV4LXBvd2VyLXVzZXItY2FtcCIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjo5fQ==.



----------------------------------------




OUR вҖҳCODEX FOR KNOWLEDGE WORKвҖҷ GUIDE GETS AN UPGRADE

Codex has been on a tear lately. With new features like Sites and role-specific plugins, and high-concept video ads scattered across San Francisco, OpenAI really wants you to know that Codex is not just for coders anymore.

According to the company, theyвҖҷre chasing promising early signals. Codex has only 5 million weekly active users overall; for comparison, ChatGPT has 900 million https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiQ29kZXggZm9yIEV2ZXJ5dGhpbmcgYW5kIEV2ZXJ5b25lIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMyMCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vb3BlbmFpLmNvbS9pbmRleC9uZXh0LXBoYXNlLW9mLWVudGVycHJpc2UtYWkvIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjEwfQ==. But knowledge workers account for about 20 percent of Codex users https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiQ29kZXggZm9yIEV2ZXJ5dGhpbmcgYW5kIEV2ZXJ5b25lIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMyMCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vb3BlbmFpLmNvbS9pbmRleC9jb2RleC1mb3Ita25vd2xlZGdlLXdvcmsvIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjExfQ==вҖ”and are growing more than three times as fast as developers. OpenAI is betting that this fast-growing group is early evidence that Codex can become the place everyone gets agentic work done, whether they identify as вҖңtechnicalвҖқ or not.

The product is evolving quickly around that bet. OpenAI has launched role-specific plugins that allow Codex to assume the expertise of a financial analyst or a product manager, and Sites to present any kind of outputs and information, technical or not. ItвҖҷs a lot to keep up withвҖ”and I say that as someone whose job is to keep up with it.

ThatвҖҷs a big part of why I think OpenAI still has an onboarding problem to solve. The people I talk to who are interested in AI but considerably less AI-pilled than I am are open to Codex. TheyвҖҷre simply unsure what they would use it for, let alone how to use it well. CodexвҖҷs flexibility is great for getting a variety of work done, but it gives new users very little guidance about where to begin.

Our вҖңCodex for Knowledge WorkвҖқ guide https://every. offers one opinionated perspective on how to get the most out of Codex.

We published the guide less than a month ago, but because so much keeps changing with Codex, we expedited an update, including:

 * A new map of how projects, threads, Goals, plugins, and Sites fit together
 * A deeper explanation of how Codex reaches your work through local files, connected apps, skills, MCP servers, browser use, and computer control
 * Stronger guidance on mobile control, team handoffs, permissions, human review, and workflow ownership
 * A 30-day plan for moving from one reliable personal workflow to more advanced uses such as plugins, Sites, and automation

This guide is a jump start for Codex, not the definitive way to use the tool. The more you work with Codex, the more you learn to ask it what it needs from youвҖ”and the more you discover it can do. One insider tip: Give the whole guide to Codex, tell it about your role and tools, and ask it to help you choose your first workflow.вҖ”Katie Parrott https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJz

----------
howdy, itвҖҷs Barsee again.

* OpenAI is taking on Nvidia with its first custom AI chip

LetвҖҷs _dive_ into the Valley of AIвҖҰ

~_*This is sponsored_~


----------

----------
#### **THROUGH THE VALLEY**

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/de24f848-2e26-45f1-bb98-85517da456c0/openai-broadcom-jalapeno-inference-chip-image__1_.png?t=1782397821)
Caption: Courtesy: OpenAI

**1/ OpenAI is taking on Nvidia with its first custom AI chip:** The company, alongside Broadcom, unveiled [JalapeГұo](https://openai.com/index/openai-broadcom-jalapeno-inference-chip/), a purpose-built inference chip designed to run ChatGPT faster and more efficiently than general-purpose GPUs. OpenAI says the chip delivers industry-leading performance per watt, marking a major step toward reducing its dependence on Nvidia while lowering long-term compute costs.

==**2/ OpenAI rolls out a new version of GPT-5.5 Instant:**== The latest version of [GPT-5.5 Instant ](https://x.com/OpenAI/status/2069843083701915755?s=20)delivers stronger conversational abilities with better intent recognition, context retention, instruction following, and adaptation to user feedback. The update also improves shopping and local recommendations, making conversations feel more natural and personalized.

**3/ Claude Fable 5 is back and rollout is going on slowly:** New Claude Code [references](https://x.com/synthwavedd/status/2069813760622043483?s=20), ongoing [White House negotiations](https://www.wired.com/story/the-trump-white-house-is-over-anthropics-dario-amodei), a federal lawsuit, and mounting congressional pressure all point to Fable 5 and Mythos moving closer to public availability. The models have remained offline since U.S. export restrictions forced Anthropic to suspend global access, but recent developments suggest a phased rollout may already be underway. Google has aВ [GitHub repo](https://github.com/google-gemini/computer-use-preview)В to try it locally.

**4/ Google is bringing AI agents directly to your computer: **The company introduced [native computer-use](https://blog.google/innovation-and-ai/models-and-research/gemini-models/introducing-computer-use-gemini-3-5-flash/?utm_source=tldrai) capabilities for Gemini 3.5 Flash, enabling the model to navigate desktop interfaces by clicking, scrolling, typing, and interacting with applications through continuous screenshots. The upgrade makes it easier for developers to build AI agents that can automate real-world computer tasks across different software.


----------

----------
# TRENDING TOOLS


--------------------
* [Notion](https://x.com/NotionHQ/status/2069816393395012009?s=20) > Introduces External Agents, bringing Claude and Cursor directly into Notion workflows

* [Exa Connect](https://x.com/ExaAILabs/status/2069842203577651283?s=20) > Gives AI agents access to private company data beyond the public web

* [OCR 4](https://mistral.ai/news/ocr-4/) > Mistral's latest OCR model with layout-aware document understanding and structured data extraction

* [Claude for Content](https://x.com/jock_ferguson/status/2069835376437682336?s=20) > Enter your website once, and Fastlane generates thousands of viral videos for your product in a single click

* [Origami](https://origami.chat/) > Describe your ideal customer once, and it finds the right buyers, recommends the best strategy, and launches email and LinkedIn outreach automatically

* [AgenticCalling](https://agenticcalling.ai/) > Lets your Claude or ChatGPT agent make real phone calls on your behalf

* [Gemini 3.5 Flash](https://blog.google/innovation-and-ai/models-and-research/gemini-models/introducing-computer-use-gemini-3-5-flash/) > Google's flagship model now supports computer use, enabling developers to build agents that can interact across apps and platforms

* [Genspark Design](https://www.genspark.ai/agents?type=design)В > Generate UI prototypes, videos, HTML animations and code


----------

----------
# WHAT I'M CONSUMING


--------------------

PLUS: AN EASY WAY TO LEVEL UP YOUR AI USAGE, AND HOW EVERYвҖҷS HEAD OF GROWTH WRITES WITH LLMS https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiVG9rZW4gVGlnaHRlbmluZyIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMTYsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL2NvbnRleHQtd2luZG93L3Rva2VuLXRpZ2h0ZW5pbmciLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6M30=

by Laura Entis https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiVG9rZW4gVGlnaHRlbmluZyIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMTYsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL0BsYXVyYV8yN2JiYWZfMSIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjo0fQ==

Midjourney/Every illustration.

AI has entered its allocation era: Companies are starting to ask who gets access to the most powerful models, what those models should be used for, and when the cost is worth it. Today, head of tech consulting Mike Taylor https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiVG9rZW4gVGlnaHRlbmluZyIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMTYsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL0BtaWtlXzIxMTQiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6NX0= argues that token budgets may start to look like trading portfolios, with the biggest compute budgets going to the people who can prove the biggest returns. Elsewhere, head of operations Arielle Shipper stress-tests her own AI habits, head of growth Austin Tedesco https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiVG9rZW4gVGlnaHRlbmluZyIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMTYsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL0B0ZWRlc2NhdSIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjo2fQ== shares how he writes with Spiral https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiVG9rZW4gVGlnaHRlbmluZyIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMTYsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL3dyaXRld2l0aHNwaXJhbC5jb20vP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZXZlcnl3ZWJzaXRlIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjd9 and Codex https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiVG9rZW4gVGlnaHRlbmluZyIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMTYsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL2NvbnRleHQtd2luZG93L2NvZGV4LW1vdmVzLWJleW9uZC1jb2RpbmciLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6OH0=, and senior designer Daniel Rodrigues https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiVG9rZW4gVGlnaHRlbmluZyIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMTYsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL0BkYW5pZWxfNWZiZDIxXzEiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6OX0= tracks the resurgence of trad design on social media.

Was this newsletter forwarded to you? Sign up https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiVG9rZW4gVGlnaHRlbmluZyIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMTYsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL2FjY291bnQiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6MTB9 to get it in your inbox.



----------------------------------------




SIGNAL

ROI COMES FOR TOKENS

Just a few months ago, heavily subsidized AI plans and a manic push by corporations to get their employees familiar with the technology led to aggressive tokenmaxxing, or measuring AI adoption by how many tokens employees use.

That era appears to be over https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiVG9rZW4gVGlnaHRlbmluZyIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMTYsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL3d3dy5ueXRpbWVzLmNvbS8yMDI2LzA2LzE4L3RlY2hub2xvZ3kvYWktdG9rZW4tbWluaW1pemluZy5odG1sIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjExfQ==. Shifting pricing models from the frontier labs, the transition from chatbots to long-running agents, and the emergence of powerful, incredibly costly https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiVG9rZW4gVGlnaHRlbmluZyIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMTYsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL2NoYWluLW9mLXRob3VnaHQvdGhlLW1vcmFsLW9mLWZhYmxlIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjEyfQ== new models have converged to create massive AI enterprise bills, often without tangible results. Uber, Meta, Amazon, and Walmart have all moved to place caps https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiVG9rZW4gVGlnaHRlbmluZyIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMTYsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL3d3dy5mdC5jb20vY29udGVudC8xZDM3Y2MwOC1lMGFhLTQ1YTQtYTQ1ZC00YWQyODI1MjkzMTQ_ on employee AI use.

These companies will still spend big on AI. But theyвҖҷre beginning to think more

strategicallyвҖ”or restrictivelyвҖ” about how to get capable, token-hungry models and workflows in the hands of people who can maximize the return on investment (ROI).

One solution is to give engineers a set percentage of their salary to spend on tokens per month. Head of tech consulting Mike Taylor thinks the financial industry offers a possible model: Capable engineers will receive multiples of their salary to manage on token spend just as financial traders manage portfolios many times the size of their annual compensation.
